item.title: Robot hand company settles Tesla trade secret suit and announces $11M raise
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Jay Li doesn’t recommend getting sued by Tesla if you’re trying to get a startup off the ground. But he does think his company, Proception, might be better off for having endured the experience. “I think it’s kind of like a resilience test, or pressure test,” he told TechCrunch in an exclusive interview. “People say […]

item.title: Pocket raises $11M in bet on rising demand for AI note-taking devices
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Pocket sells a $129 credit card-shaped puck, which sticks to the back of your phone, and promises unlimited recordings, transcriptions, and to-do items.

item.title: Flipper Device’s new Busy Bar is a customizable display for productivity
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Flipper Device's new Busy Bar will retail for $249.

item.title: Omen AI’s plan to optimize data centers is all wet
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Omen AI raised a $31 million Series A to monitor chip coolant and stop bacterial outbreaks in data centers.

item.title: California law targeting loud streaming ads takes effect on July 1
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Streaming ads might be getting a lot quieter.

item.title: Ford rehires ‘gray beard’ engineers after AI falls short
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: "Mistakenly we thought that by just introducing artificial intelligence ... that would produce a high-quality product.”

item.title: Writer Ian Bogost says ‘The Small Stuff’ can help us reclaim our lives from too much convenience
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Has Silicon Valley been building the wrong things?

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: All eyes on Tesla FSD
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

item.title: Why Wall Street thinks US memory maker Micron is the next Nvidia
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Eager to find more public AI-related companies that may do as well as Nvidia, Wall Street investors think they've found a winner with Micron.

item.title: Govee’s smart nugget ice maker makes every iced drink feel like a luxury
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.category: goveelife smart nugget ice maker pro
item.description: For some people, the ice in a beverage is almost as important as the drink itself. That’s the audience Govee had in mind when designing its latest ice maker, the GoveeLife Smart Nugget Ice Maker Pro. This $500 premium smart home gadget is aimed at those who crave what’s called “the good ice,” the soft, chewable […]

item.title: Indian payments chief thinks AI will be heavily involved in next era of digital payment growth
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Dilip Asbe said that newer UPI apps could be more competitive with a viable commercial model

item.title: Instagram is testing more ways to customize ‘Your Algorithm’
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Instagram users could soon see more ways to tune their content.

item.title: SoftBank’s CEO isn’t the only one with questions about Elon Musk’s orbital data center hype
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Not everyone is buying Elon Musk’s vision for orbital data centers.

item.title: Apple Vision Pro exec is reportedly leaving for OpenAI
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Paul Meade, the Apple vice president in charge of the Vision Pro headset, is reportedly leaving the company to join OpenAI’s hardware team.

item.title: The fittest founder in the room got cancer. Here’s how he used AI to fight back.
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: When confronted with cancer, Connor Christou fed everything tied tied to his regime — blood results, scan data, wearable output, journal entries — into Claude.

item.title: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like models as Anthropic’s export ban drags on
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: New models are launching in Asia that promise Mythos-like capabilities without fear of an export ban. U.S. AI labs may never recover this enormous market.

item.title: Trump Admin releases Anthropic Mythos to be used by more than 100 US companies, agencies
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.

item.title: FTC gives Musk the OK to acquire SpaceX alumni startup Mesh
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Mesh came out of stealth in February with a $50 million Series A.

item.title: Corgi, the buzzy Y Combinator-backed insurance tech startup, says it didn’t steal an open source product
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Corgi became embroiled in controversy when Papermark accused it of stealing its software. Corgi says it did not, raising new questions about vibe coding.

item.title: Novak Djokovic has a new job — advisor to private equity firm General Atlantic
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: General Atlantic has tapped tennis legend Novak Djokovic to serve as a global strategic advisor.
